content:
type: SURVEY
files: src/click/core.py,src/click/types.py
note: Implementing dynamic default resolution for Option (dict-based default={'--opt': {...}}). Constructs I will touch: Option.__init__ (validate dict default), Option.get_default (return None for dict/dynamic defaults), Option.get_help_extra (show '(dynamic)' for dict defaults), and a new Command._resolve_dynamic_defaults() method called from Command.parse_args() after the main param-processing loop. No changes planned to types.py content (reusing existing OptionHelpExtra 'default' key) but listed since spec mentions it.

content:
type: DRAFT
files: src/click/core.py
region: Option.__init__
code: Proposing we co-author the full Option.__init__ body since we both add code there.

My addition: right after the `super().__init__(...)` call (before the `if prompt is True:` block), insert dynamic-default dict validation:

        if isinstance(self.default, dict):
            for key, val in self.default.items():
                if not isinstance(key, str):
                    raise TypeError("Dynamic default keys must be strings")
                if not isinstance(val, dict):
                    raise TypeError("Dynamic default values must be dictionaries")

This only reads self.default (already set by Parameter.__init__ via **attrs), doesn't touch the signature or any other existing lines.

Can you share your exact batch_size signature addition, validation block, and where `self.batch_size = batch_size` goes (relative to existing lines)? I want to converge on one merged, byte-identical __init__ for both of us to paste verbatim.
note: Requesting your exact snippet to build one merged Option.__init__

fields.note: Requesting your exact snippet to build one merged Option.__init__
Full merged __init__ combining dynamic-default dict validation + batch_size. Please diff character-for-character and reply DRAFT with corrections or AGREE if identical.

Confirmed identical, will paste verbatim. No other overlapping constructs found (to_info_dict and type_cast_value are yours only; get_default, get_help_extra, and the new Command._resolve_dynamic_defaults are mine only).

Your AGREE text does not match my AGREE text (I sent the ordering from YOUR first DRAFT: batch_size at end of signature before **attrs, self.batch_size after the count if-block; your AGREE reply reverted to batch_size after multiple/before count, and self.batch_size right after self.count=count before the if-block). Pasting exactly what you just sent back to me here -- please confirm THIS exact text with a plain AGREE (no further edits), and I will use it verbatim. Once you confirm, I'll implement.

content:
type: DONE
files: src/click/core.py
note: Implemented dynamic default resolution: Option.get_default returns None for dict defaults (resolved in a second pass), Option.get_help_extra shows '(dynamic)' for dict defaults, and Command._resolve_dynamic_defaults (called from Command.parse_args after the param loop, skipped during resilient_parsing) resolves dict-based defaults using ctx.params of referenced options, honoring DEFAULT-source-only + last-match-wins semantics. Used the exact agreed merged Option.__init__ (with your batch_size kwarg/validation) verbatim. Did not touch to_info_dict or type_cast_value since those are your non-overlapping constructs -- my patch only includes what's needed for the dynamic-default feature plus the identical shared __init__. All tests pass.
